task_completion.description: Log into the Splunk Enterprise web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk web interface is visible in a browser at localhost:8000, logged in (e.g., 'Administrator' or 'admin' visible in the top navigation bar).
A dashboard with the exact title 'Splunk_Platform_Health' is visible in the dashboard editor, view mode, or the Dashboards list in the Search & Reporting app.

task_completion.description: The dashboard contains at least 4 panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Splunk_Platform_Health' dashboard displays 4 or more distinct visualization panels or search inputs.

task_completion.description: Dashboard panels query Splunk internal indexes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SPL queries visible in the dashboard editor, search bar, or panel configurations explicitly include 'index=_internal', 'index=_introspection', or 'index=_audit'.
A saved search or report with the exact name 'Splunk_Indexing_Anomaly_Detection' is visible in the 'Save As' dialog, the Reports list, or the Searches list.

task_completion.description: The saved search uses a valid SPL query against internal indexes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar or saved search configuration for 'Splunk_Indexing_Anomaly_Detection' shows an SPL query containing 'index=_internal', 'index=_introspection', or 'index=_audit'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk Web GUI to complete the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the Splunk UI, rather than a terminal window editing .conf files directly.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed searches and built the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Splunk UI elements like search results, event counts, job status indicators (green checkmarks), or rendered visualizations are visible, proving the queries were run.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the answers in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The expected names and queries are entered into the actual Splunk application fields (search bar, dashboard title, save dialogs), not just typed into a notepad or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Splunk_Platform_Health' and it must contain a minimum of 4 panels. The expected saved search name is exactly 'Splunk_Indexing_Anomaly_Detection'. Both the dashboard panels and the saved search must use SPL queries that reference Splunk's internal indexes (specifically '_internal', '_introspection', or '_audit'). The Splunk web interface is accessible at http://localhost:8000 with credentials admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
